monday.question: What does 'opinion' mean?
options: A proven fact about something
A personal view or belief about something
A question you ask someone
A rule that everyone must follow
monday.correctAnswer: A personal view or belief about something

monday.question: What does 'agree' mean?
options: To have the same view or feeling as someone else
To argue with someone about something
To change your mind about something
To explain your idea in detail
monday.correctAnswer: To have the same view or feeling as someone else

monday.question: What does 'disagree' mean?
options: To have the same opinion as someone
To repeat what someone said
To have a different opinion from someone
To ask someone to explain better
monday.correctAnswer: To have a different opinion from someone

monday.question: What does 'perspective' mean?
options: A way of thinking about or seeing something based on your experience
A type of question used in debates
A formal written opinion
A word used only in academic writing
monday.correctAnswer: A way of thinking about or seeing something based on your experience

monday.question: What does 'argument' mean in an academic or discussion context?
options: A fight or physical conflict between people
A reason or set of reasons given to support a point of view
A question that has no answer
A sentence with no main verb
monday.correctAnswer: A reason or set of reasons given to support a point of view

monday.question: What does 'point of view' mean?
options: The place where you are standing
A type of grammar structure
A particular way of thinking about something
A sentence that starts with 'I'
monday.correctAnswer: A particular way of thinking about something

monday.question: What does 'believe' mean?
options: To know something for certain based on evidence
To think that something is true without absolute proof
To change your mind after discussion
To understand something fully
monday.correctAnswer: To think that something is true without absolute proof

monday.question: Which phrase is used to introduce a personal opinion?
options: In my opinion, ...
As a matter of fact, ...
According to the news, ...
It is known that ...
monday.correctAnswer: In my opinion, ...

monday.question: Which of the following is an opinion phrase?
options: I believe that technology improves education.
Technology was invented in the 20th century.
Many people use technology every day.
Technology includes computers and phones.
monday.correctAnswer: I believe that technology improves education.

monday.question: Which sentence correctly expresses an opinion?
options: From my perspective, remote work is more productive.
From my perspective remote work is more productive
In my opinion remote work is more productively.
I perspective think remote work is more productive.
monday.correctAnswer: From my perspective, remote work is more productive.

monday.question: Which phrase means 'In my opinion' in a more formal way?
options: I guess ...
As far as I'm concerned, ...
I think maybe ...
It's possible that ...
monday.correctAnswer: As far as I'm concerned, ...

monday.question: Complete: '_____ my opinion, social media has both advantages and disadvantages.'
monday.correctAnswer: In

monday.question: Complete: 'I _____ that everyone should learn a second language.'
monday.correctAnswer: believe|think|feel

monday.question: Complete: 'From my _____, the city needs more public parks.'
monday.correctAnswer: perspective|point of view

monday.question: Complete: 'As far as I'm _____, working from home is better for families.'
monday.correctAnswer: concerned

monday.question: '_____ my perspective, learning English opens many opportunities in life.'
monday.correctAnswer: From

monday.question: She expressed her _____ clearly during the class debate.
monday.correctAnswer: opinion|point of view|perspective|argument

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Na minha opinião, a educação é muito importante.'
monday.correctAnswer: in my opinion, education is very important

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu acredito que o trabalho remoto é mais eficiente.'
monday.correctAnswer: i believe that remote work is more efficient

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Do meu ponto de vista, essa é uma boa ideia.'
monday.correctAnswer: from my point of view, that is a good idea

tuesday.question: What does 'absolutely' mean when used to agree with someone?
options: Maybe, I'm not sure
I completely agree, without any doubt
I understand but I disagree
That's an interesting point
tuesday.correctAnswer: I completely agree, without any doubt

tuesday.question: What does 'valid' mean when talking about an argument or opinion?
options: Rude or disrespectful
Impossible to prove
Well-reasoned and acceptable
Based on feelings, not facts
tuesday.correctAnswer: Well-reasoned and acceptable

tuesday.question: Which sentence expresses agreement?
options: I couldn't agree more with your point.
I'm not sure that's right.
I see it differently.
I don't think so.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I couldn't agree more with your point.

tuesday.question: Which sentence expresses disagreement politely?
options: You're completely wrong about that.
I see your point, but I disagree.
That's absolutely right!
I couldn't agree more.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I see your point, but I disagree.

tuesday.question: What does 'I couldn't agree more.' mean?
options: I partially agree with you.
I completely agree with you.
I don't agree with you at all.
I need more time to think.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I completely agree with you.

tuesday.question: Which response best shows disagreement?
options: Absolutely! That's a great point.
You're right, I agree completely.
I'm not sure about that. I think it depends.
Exactly! That's what I think too.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I'm not sure about that. I think it depends.

tuesday.question: Which phrase introduces a contrasting opinion?
options: On the other hand, ...
In addition, ...
For example, ...
As a result, ...
tuesday.correctAnswer: On the other hand, ...

tuesday.question: Which is the most polite way to disagree?
options: That's wrong!
You don't know what you're talking about.
With all due respect, I see it differently.
No, you're incorrect.
tuesday.correctAnswer: With all due respect, I see it differently.

tuesday.question: What does 'That's a fair point.' signal in a conversation?
options: Disagreement with the speaker
Acknowledging that the other person made a good argument
Changing the subject
Asking for more information
tuesday.correctAnswer: Acknowledging that the other person made a good argument

tuesday.question: Which phrase is used to agree strongly?
options: I'm not entirely sure...
Exactly! That's what I think too.
Well, it depends on the situation.
I don't think that's right.
tuesday.correctAnswer: Exactly! That's what I think too.

tuesday.question: Complete: 'I _____ with you completely. That's a great idea.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: agree

tuesday.question: Complete: 'I see your point, _____ I still think there's a better solution.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: but

tuesday.question: Complete: 'On the _____ hand, some people believe that technology creates more problems.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: other

tuesday.question: Complete: 'With all due _____, I don't think that argument is valid.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: respect

tuesday.question: Complete: 'That's a _____ point. I hadn't thought about it that way before.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: good|fair|valid|great

tuesday.question: 'I couldn't _____ more. That is exactly what I was thinking.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: agree

tuesday.question: I understand your opinion, _____ I have a different point of view.
tuesday.correctAnswer: but|however|although

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu concordo completamente com você.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: i completely agree with you|i agree with you completely

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu discordo. Acho que é mais complicado do que isso.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: i disagree. i think it is more complicated than that

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Por outro lado, há pessoas que pensam de forma diferente.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: on the other hand, there are people who think differently

wednesday.question: What does 'convince' mean?
options: To make someone believe or agree with you
To repeat your opinion louder
To avoid expressing an opinion
To change the subject in a debate
wednesday.correctAnswer: To make someone believe or agree with you

wednesday.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'I think that'?
options: I think that public transport should be free.
I think that public transport should to be free.
I am thinking that public transport should free.
I think that public transport are free.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I think that public transport should be free.

wednesday.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'I believe'?
options: I believe learning languages is a great skill.
I believe to learn languages is a great skill.
I am believe that languages is a great skill.
I believe languages are great skill.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I believe learning languages is a great skill.

wednesday.question: Choose the correct sentence with 'In my opinion':
options: In my opinion, the movie was too long.
In my opinion the movie it was too long.
In my opinion, the movie were too long.
In my opinion: the movie too long.
wednesday.correctAnswer: In my opinion, the movie was too long.

wednesday.question: Which structure is used to express a personal opinion?
options: As far as I'm concerned, ...
According to scientists, ...
It has been proven that ...
Research shows that ...
wednesday.correctAnswer: As far as I'm concerned, ...

wednesday.question: Which word CANNOT be used to introduce an opinion?
options: believe
think
feel
drive
wednesday.correctAnswer: drive

wednesday.question: Which sentence uses an opinion phrase incorrectly?
options: In my opinion, reading books is better than watching TV.
I believe that exercise is important for health.
From my perspective, the city needs more green areas.
In my opinion of the weather is nice today.
wednesday.correctAnswer: In my opinion of the weather is nice today.

wednesday.question: What does 'I feel that ...' signal?
options: A scientific fact
A personal emotional belief or opinion
Something that someone else said
A neutral description
wednesday.correctAnswer: A personal emotional belief or opinion

wednesday.question: Which phrase acknowledges the other side before disagreeing?
options: I see your point, but ...
You are wrong because ...
That's absolutely right.
I couldn't agree more.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I see your point, but ...

wednesday.question: Which option correctly disagrees with: 'I think social media is great.'?
options: I don't think so. It can be harmful to mental health.
I couldn't agree more. Social media is wonderful.
Absolutely! I use it every day.
That's true. I totally agree.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I don't think so. It can be harmful to mental health.

wednesday.question: Which expression is used to soften a disagreement?
options: That may be true, but ...
You're completely wrong!
I totally agree!
Exactly right!
wednesday.correctAnswer: That may be true, but ...

wednesday.question: Which sentence shows a balanced opinion?
options: Technology has advantages, but it also has disadvantages.
Technology is always good.
Technology is always bad.
Technology has nothing to do with education.
wednesday.correctAnswer: Technology has advantages, but it also has disadvantages.

wednesday.question: Complete: 'I _____ that people should exercise at least three times a week.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: think|believe|feel

wednesday.question: Complete: 'That _____ be true, but I still think we need to consider other options.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: may

wednesday.question: Complete: '_____ my perspective, the government should invest more in education.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: From

wednesday.question: Complete: 'I _____ your point, but I see the situation differently.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: see|understand|get

wednesday.question: In my opinion, _____ should be taught in every school.
wednesday.correctAnswer: English|music|art|sports|financial education

wednesday.question: That's a _____ argument. I hadn't considered that before.
wednesday.correctAnswer: good|valid|strong|fair

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'Do meu ponto de vista, as crianças assistem televisão demais.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: from my point of view, children watch too much television

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'Entendo o seu ponto, mas discordo.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: i understand your point, but i disagree|i see your point, but i disagree

thursday.question: What does 'neutral' mean in a debate context?
options: Strongly in favor of one side
Not supporting either side; unbiased
Completely against an idea
Giving a very long opinion
thursday.correctAnswer: Not supporting either side; unbiased

thursday.question: What does 'acknowledge' mean when responding to someone's opinion?
options: To completely reject what someone said
To recognize or accept the truth of what someone said
To repeat someone's opinion back to them
To change the topic of a conversation
thursday.correctAnswer: To recognize or accept the truth of what someone said

thursday.question: Which sentence correctly responds to: 'I think we should work less.'?
options: I agree. Working less could improve our well-being.
I agrees. Working less could improve our well-being.
I agreeing with you. Working less could improve our well-being.
I am agree. Working less could improve our well-being.
thursday.correctAnswer: I agree. Working less could improve our well-being.

thursday.question: Which is NOT a phrase used to disagree?
options: I don't think so.
I see it differently.
I'm not convinced.
I couldn't agree more.
thursday.correctAnswer: I couldn't agree more.

thursday.question: Which is NOT a phrase used to agree?
options: That's true.
Absolutely!
I'm not sure about that.
You're right.
thursday.correctAnswer: I'm not sure about that.

thursday.question: In which situation would you say 'I see your point, but...'?
options: When you completely agree with someone
When you want to end the conversation
When you partially agree but want to offer a different view
When you don't understand what the person said
thursday.correctAnswer: When you partially agree but want to offer a different view

thursday.question: Which sentence is a correct example of disagreement using 'however'?
options: Solar energy is clean. However, it can be expensive to install.
Solar energy is clean, however is expensive to install.
However solar energy is clean, expensive.
Solar energy is clean. It however expensive.
thursday.correctAnswer: Solar energy is clean. However, it can be expensive to install.

thursday.question: Which sentence uses 'although' correctly to show a contrasting opinion?
options: Although I understand your view, I think differently.
Although I understand your view but I think differently.
I understand your view, although but I think differently.
Although, I understand your view.
thursday.correctAnswer: Although I understand your view, I think differently.

thursday.question: What is the function of 'On the other hand' in a discussion?
options: To introduce an additional similar point
To introduce a contrasting or opposing point
To give an example
To conclude the argument
thursday.correctAnswer: To introduce a contrasting or opposing point

thursday.question: Which phrase shows the speaker is not completely convinced?
options: Absolutely, I agree!
That's exactly what I think.
I'm not entirely sure about that.
You're totally right!
thursday.correctAnswer: I'm not entirely sure about that.

thursday.question: Choose the best response to: 'In my opinion, cars should be banned in city centers.'
options: I agree. It would reduce pollution and traffic.
I agreeing because it reduce pollution.
I am agree. It reduces pollution.
I agrees that it would reduce pollution.
thursday.correctAnswer: I agree. It would reduce pollution and traffic.

thursday.question: Which phrase signals that you are about to give your opinion?
options: The fact is that ...
Research proves that ...
As far as I'm concerned, ...
It is well-known that ...
thursday.correctAnswer: As far as I'm concerned, ...

thursday.question: Complete: '_____ I agree with some of your ideas, I think the plan needs more work.'
thursday.correctAnswer: Although|While|Even though

thursday.question: Complete: 'Pets are great companions. _____, they require a lot of time and money.'
thursday.correctAnswer: However|On the other hand|That said

thursday.question: Complete: 'I'm not _____ convinced that this is the best approach.'
thursday.correctAnswer: entirely|completely|totally

thursday.question: Complete: 'I _____ your point, but from my perspective, there are other factors to consider.'
thursday.correctAnswer: see|understand|acknowledge

thursday.question: 'Do you agree with the idea of working from home?' — 'To be honest, I'm not _____ sure. It has pros and cons.'
thursday.correctAnswer: entirely|completely|totally

thursday.question: She made a _____ argument and everyone in the room changed their opinion.
thursday.correctAnswer: convincing|strong|valid|powerful

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Embora eu entenda seu ponto de vista, discordo.'
thursday.correctAnswer: although i understand your point of view, i disagree

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'No que me diz respeito, essa é a melhor solução.'
thursday.correctAnswer: as far as i'm concerned, that is the best solution

friday.question: What does 'biased' mean in the context of an opinion or argument?
options: Based on solid evidence and facts
Unfairly influenced by personal feelings or preferences
Very well-balanced and neutral
Extremely polite and formal
friday.correctAnswer: Unfairly influenced by personal feelings or preferences

friday.question: Which sentence is the most complete and well-structured opinion?
options: In my opinion, electric cars are better for the environment because they produce less pollution.
I think electric cars good.
Electric cars opinion better.
From perspective, electric cars.
friday.correctAnswer: In my opinion, electric cars are better for the environment because they produce less pollution.

friday.question: Which sentence correctly combines an opinion with a reason?
options: I believe that reading is important because it improves vocabulary and critical thinking.
I believe that reading is important because it improve vocabulary.
I am believing reading important because vocabulary.
I believe reading important it improves vocabulary.
friday.correctAnswer: I believe that reading is important because it improves vocabulary and critical thinking.

friday.question: Which response shows partial agreement followed by a contrasting point?
options: That's a good point. However, we also need to consider the costs.
I completely disagree with everything you said.
Absolutely! I agree 100%.
I have no opinion on this topic.
friday.correctAnswer: That's a good point. However, we also need to consider the costs.

friday.question: What is wrong with: 'I am disagree with your opinion.'?
options: Nothing — it is correct.
'Disagree' cannot follow 'am'. The correct form is 'I disagree'.
'Opinion' should be 'opinions'.
'Your' should be 'you'.
friday.correctAnswer: 'Disagree' cannot follow 'am'. The correct form is 'I disagree'.

friday.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'however' to contrast two ideas?
options: Fast food is cheap and convenient. However, it is not very healthy.
Fast food is cheap and convenient however it is not very healthy.
However fast food is cheap, convenient.
Fast food is cheap. Convenient however.
friday.correctAnswer: Fast food is cheap and convenient. However, it is not very healthy.

friday.question: Which phrase is the MOST formal way to express an opinion?
options: I guess ...
I kind of think ...
As far as I'm concerned, ...
I dunno, but ...
friday.correctAnswer: As far as I'm concerned, ...

friday.question: Which sentence correctly agrees AND adds a new idea?
options: I agree, and I would also add that regular exercise improves mental health.
I agree, but I would also add that exercise improves mental health.
Both A and B are correct.
Neither A nor B are correct.
friday.correctAnswer: Both A and B are correct.

friday.question: A classmate says: 'I think fast food restaurants should be closed.' The most respectful way to disagree is:
options: That's completely wrong and a terrible idea.
I respect your opinion, but I think banning them would affect many jobs.
You don't know what you're talking about.
I don't care about your opinion.
friday.correctAnswer: I respect your opinion, but I think banning them would affect many jobs.

friday.question: Which is a complete and correct expression of disagreement?
options: I don't think so. In my opinion, the situation is more complex than that.
Not agree. The situation complex.
I no agree, situation is complex.
Not thinking so, the situation is complexer.
friday.correctAnswer: I don't think so. In my opinion, the situation is more complex than that.

friday.question: What is the function of 'To be fair, ...' in a discussion?
options: To start a disagreement
To acknowledge something positive in the opposing view
To end the conversation
To repeat your own opinion
friday.correctAnswer: To acknowledge something positive in the opposing view

friday.question: Which pair of expressions shows agreement and then a contrasting opinion?
options: That's true. On the other hand, ...
I disagree. For example, ...
You're right. In addition, ...
I don't think so. However, ...
friday.correctAnswer: That's true. On the other hand, ...

friday.question: Complete: 'I _____ believe that children spend too much time on screens.'
friday.correctAnswer: strongly|truly|firmly|personally

friday.question: Complete: 'To be _____, I think both sides have valid points in this debate.'
friday.correctAnswer: fair|honest

friday.question: Complete: 'I _____ your point of view, and I think you raise some important issues.'
friday.correctAnswer: respect|understand|acknowledge

friday.question: Complete: 'She didn't _____ with my idea, but she was polite about it.'
friday.correctAnswer: agree

friday.question: I believe that _____, but I understand that others may see it differently.
friday.correctAnswer: education is essential|everyone should learn a language|exercise is important

friday.question: He made a very _____ argument, and I found it hard to disagree.
friday.correctAnswer: convincing|valid|strong|compelling

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Na minha opinião, aprender inglês muda a sua vida.'
friday.correctAnswer: in my opinion, learning english changes your life

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Respeito sua opinião, mas penso de forma diferente.'
friday.correctAnswer: i respect your opinion, but i think differently
